title: bursaria spinosa subsp. lasiophylla
intro: Bursaria spinosa subsp. lasiophylla, also known as the 'Hairy Bursaria', is a shrub or small tree native to eastern Australia, belonging to the family Pittosporaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily its leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The leaves of Bursaria spinosa subsp. lasiophylla are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant, including teas and infusions, are commonly used in traditional medicine, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
